ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC. - FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. FIREMAN'S FUND IS ALLOWED TO BRING SAID MOTION. (SEE WESTERN HERITAGE INS. CO. V. SUPERIOR COURT (2011) 199 CAL.APP.4TH 1196.) THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA ON OCTOBER 16, 2014 IS SET ASIDE PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S INHERENT, EQUITABLE POWER TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT ON THE GROUND OF EXTRINSIC FRAUD OR MISTAKE. THE COURT FURTHER ORDERED CONTINUED THE HEARING TO OCTOBER 11, 2016 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503 TO ALLOW PARTIES TO MEET AND CONFER ON A PROPOSED ORDER RE: FIREMAN'S FUND'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA ON OCTOBER 23, 2012. PLAINTIFF EXERCISED A 170.6 AS TO JUDGE MARLA MILLER AND THE COURT ACCEPTED THE CHALLENGE AS TIMELY. DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC EXERCISED A 170.6 CCP PEREMPTORY CHALLENGE AS TO JUDGE NEWTON LAM. THE COURT ACCEPTED THE CHALLENGE AS TIMELY. THE COURT ORDERED PARTIES TO HAVE A LIMIT OF 5 MOTIONS IN LIMINE PER EACH SIDE THAT ARE NOT TO EXCEED 5 PAGES. THE PLAINTIFF IS ALSO REQUESTING DEFENDANTS TO HAVE A COMPREHENSIVE INDEX. IF A DEFENDANT NEEDS A CASE SPECIFIC OR DEFENDANT SPECIFIC MIL IN ADDITION TO THE 5 MIL'S THAT ARE BEING FILED JOINTLY, PARTIES NEED TO MEET AND CONFER WITH FELLOW CO-DEFENDANTS AND THEN MEET AND CONFER WITH PLAINTIFF, THEN SUBMIT A DECLARATION BY 3/28/14 AT 9:00AM IN DEPT. 503 WITH ALL OF THE MOTIONS IN LIMINE FILED AS WELL. PARTIES NEED TO WORK ON THE PAGE LINE DESIGNATIONS AND SUBMIT A JOINT LETTER TO THE COURT REGARDING THE STATUS. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON; CLERK: RVELUZ; COURT REPORTER: JENNIFER SEELENBACHER, CSR#11878, (510)451-1580. (503)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard Lee of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, the insurer of defendant Associated Insurance of California, Inc., a suspended corporation
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party William Hogan

RULING:
FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside default entered against Associated Insulation of California is denied. And FFIC's motion to set aside default judgment entered against Associated Insulation of California, Inc. is granted. Order signed in open court. Refer to the signed order and reporter's transcript for details of ruling. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment Entered Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kyle J. Clawson of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), specially appearing for intervenor / moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company, the insurer of defendant Associated Insurance of California, Inc., a suspended corporation
Michael D. Levinson of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party William Hogan

RULING:
INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY, THE INSURER OF DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSURANCE OF CALIFORNIA, INC., A SUSPENDED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on October 16, 2014 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The Court further ordered continued the hearing to October 11, 2016 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503 to allow parties to meet and confer on a proposed order re: Fireman's Fund's motion to set aside the default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on October 23, 2012. Judge: Garrett L. Fireman's Fund's motion to set aside the default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on October 23, 2012 Wong, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation Of California
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorfman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Sonia Wong, CSR#11288.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, WILLIAM HOGAN.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiff WILLIAM HOGAN shall recover damages from defendant ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA in the amount of $50,000.00 in Economic Damages, $300,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $350,000.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.
